post.description:
OAuth2 compatible token login, get an access token for future requests.

Validates:
- User exists and not soft-deleted
- Password correct
- User is active

Refresh access token using a valid refresh token.

Validates:
- Token is valid and type is 'refresh'
- User still exists and not soft-deleted
- User is still active

Create new user.

Validates:
- Email not already registered (including soft-deleted users)
- User data meets schema requirements
- Supabase Auth identity is created before local domain records

get.description:
Get current authenticated user.

Updates last_login timestamp on every authenticated call.
Dependency ensures:
- User is authenticated
- User is active
- User is not soft-deleted
List agencies for admin review, optionally filtered by status.

schema.title: Response Get Admin Agencies Api V1 Admin Agencies  Get

patch.description:
Approve a pending agency and promote the applicant atomically.

The applicant is resolved by agencies.owner_email. If Supabase Auth metadata
sync fails, the transaction is rolled back so local DB role and external JWT
claims cannot drift.
Reject a pending agency application with a required audit reason.
Revoke an approved agency back to pending review with a required reason.
Suspend an agency without soft-deleting its data, requiring a reason.

get.description:
Retrieve users with pagination and optional role/activity_state filters (admin only).

Returns only non-deleted users (deleted_at IS NULL).

schema.description: Filter by role: seeker, agent, agency_owner, admin
schema.title: Role

schema.title: Activity State

schema.title: Response Get Users Counts Api V1 Admin Users Counts  Get

get.description:
Get user by ID (admin only).

Returns 404 if user not found or soft-deleted.

put.description:
Update user (admin only).

Tracks admin who made the update via updated_by (Supabase UUID).
updated_at handled automatically by DB trigger.

delete.description:
Soft delete user (admin only).

Sets deleted_at timestamp and tracks who deleted via deleted_by.
User data preserved for audit trail, FK relationships intact.

get.description:
Get agency memberships for a specific user (admin only).

Returns all non-deleted memberships for the given user with agency name.
Non-admin JWT returns 403. Missing user_id returns 404.
User with no memberships returns empty list.

schema.title: Response Get User Memberships Api V1 Admin Users  User Id  Memberships  Get

post.description:
Restore a soft-deleted user (admin only).

Clears deleted_at to restore access. deleted_by audit record is preserved.
Tracks who restored via updated_by.

Soft delete a user (admin only).

Sets deleted_at to revoke access. Use DELETE /users/{user_id} for the
standard soft delete path. This endpoint exists for explicit admin
deactivation with the same audit trail.
Tracks who deactivated via updated_by (written to deleted_by as well).

patch.description:
Platform-level deactivation (admin only).

Sets is_active = false on the target user. Unlike soft-delete (POST
.../deactivate), the user record remains visible but all API access
is blocked by the get_current_active_user dependency.
Writes deactivation_reason and updated_by for audit.

Platform-level reactivation (admin only).

Sets is_active = true on the target user, restoring API access.
The deactivation_reason is preserved for audit.

get.description:
Retrieve properties with pagination (admin only).

Returns only non-deleted properties (deleted_at IS NULL).
CRUD layer enforces soft delete filtering.

The admin dashboard only needs the schema-backed listing fields, so we
serialize through `PropertyResponse` instead of exposing the raw ORM model.
That avoids production-only encoding failures from the database geometry
column while keeping the response shape stable for the frontend.

Query params:
- moderation_status: filter by moderation status (e.g. agency_approved for queue).

schema.title: Response Get Properties Api V1 Admin Properties Get

delete.description:
Soft delete property (admin only).

Sets deleted_at timestamp and tracks who deleted via deleted_by.
Property data preserved for audit trail, FK relationships intact.

post.description:
Mark a property as verified.

Tracks who verified via updated_by.

put.description:
Approve a property listing (admin only).

Sets is_approved = true and tracks who approved.

Update an agency. Admins can update any agency; agency owners can update their own public profile fields.

delete.description:
Soft delete an agency. Admin only.

CRITICAL: This is a multi-tenant hub operation.
Sets deleted_at timestamp, preserves all data.

Considerations:
- Agents belonging to this agency remain (FK preserved)
- Properties remain (FK preserved)
- Consider business rules: prevent deletion if active agents/properties exist

Audit: Tracks who deleted via deleted_by (Supabase UUID).

patch.description:
Update the public-verification state of a property listing.

Permissions (three-tier moderation):
- Admins can set any moderation status; verifying to 'verified'
  is only allowed from 'agency_approved' status.
- Agency owners and owning agents can return their listing to
  pending_review, but can no longer directly publish.

This endpoint exists separately from the general update endpoint so the UI
can expose a clear "verification" action without asking operators to edit
raw listing fields or fall back to manual SQL.
Agent submits a draft listing for agency review (draft → agency_review).
Agency owner submits a draft listing directly to admin review (draft → admin_review).
Agency owner approves a listing for admin review (agency_review → admin_review).
Agency owner rejects a listing back to the agent (agency_review → agency_rejected).
Agent withdraws a listing from agency review (agency_review → draft).
Agent resubmits a previously agency-rejected listing (agency_rejected → agency_review).

Agency owner recalls a listing from admin review.

- Own listing: admin_review → draft (bypass agency_review)
- Agent's listing: admin_review → agency_review
Admin rejects a listing from admin review (admin_review → admin_rejected).
Admin reinstates a listing to admin review (admin_rejected → admin_review).
Admin revokes a live listing (live → revoked).

Admin permanently rejects a revoked listing (revoked → admin_rejected).
After this, the standard admin_rejected mediation rules apply:
agency must instruct before agent can act.
Admin restores a revoked listing to live (revoked → live).

Agent pulls back a revoked or admin-rejected listing to draft.

Legal transitions via the central guard:
- revoked → draft
- admin_rejected → draft

Agency owner writes instruction to agent for a revoked/rejected listing.

The instruction is tied to the most recent revocation/rejection event so that
instructions from a prior lifecycle cycle do not unlock CTAs in a new cycle.

delete.description: Cancel a pending join request owned by the authenticated user.

get.description: Return agency join requests submitted by the authenticated seeker.

schema.title: Response Read My Join Requests Api V1 Join Requests Mine  Get

post.description:
Create a new saved search for the current user.

Search criteria stored as JSONB for flexible filtering.
Audit: Tracks creator via user_id FK.

get.description:
Retrieve all saved searches for the current user.

Returns only active (non-deleted) searches.
CRUD layer enforces deleted_at IS NULL filtering.

schema.title: Response Read User Saved Searches Api V1 Saved Searches  Get

get.description:
Public one-click unsubscribe for saved-search match emails.

No authentication is required because the UUID token is the capability.
The table uses soft delete as its active/deactivated state.

Get a specific saved search by ID.

Users can only access their own searches (unless admin).

put.description:
Update a saved search owned by the current user.

Audit: No updated_by for saved_searches per schema.

delete.description:
Soft delete a saved search owned by the current user.

Sets deleted_at timestamp, preserves data for audit trail.
Audit: Tracks who deleted via deleted_by (Supabase UUID).

post.description:
Execute a saved search and return matching properties.

Applies the stored JSONB search criteria to current property data.
Users can only execute their own searches (unless admin).
Max limit: 1000 results per execution.

schema.title: Response Execute Saved Search Api V1 Saved Searches  Search Id  Execute Post

post.description:
Create a new property inquiry.

- Validates property exists
- Associates inquiry with current user
- Property owner can be notified via background task

Audit: Tracks inquirer via user_id FK

get.description:
Retrieve all inquiries made by the current user.

Returns only non-deleted inquiries (deleted_at IS NULL).

schema.title: Response Read User Inquiries Api V1 Inquiries  Get

get.description:
Retrieve all inquiries for properties owned by the current user.

- Only agents and admins can access
- Returns inquiries for all properties where user_id matches

schema.title: Response Read Received Inquiries Api V1 Inquiries Received Get

get.description:
Get inquiry by ID.

- Inquiry creator can view
- Property owner can view
- Admins can view

put.description:
Update an inquiry (typically to mark as responded or update message).

- Inquiry creator can update their message
- Property owner can update response/status
- Admins can update any field

Audit: updated_at handled by DB trigger

delete.description:
Soft delete an inquiry.

- Only inquiry creator or admin can delete
- Sets deleted_at timestamp

Audit: Tracks who deleted via deleted_by (Supabase UUID)

get.description:
Retrieve all inquiries for a specific property.

- Only property owner or admin can view
- Returns non-deleted inquiries

schema.title: Response Read Inquiries By Property Api V1 Inquiries By Property  Property Id  Get

patch.description:
Update inquiry status (new, viewed, responded).

- Property owner can update status
- Admins can update any inquiry status

post.description:
Mark inquiry as viewed.

- Property owner can mark as viewed
- Admins can mark any inquiry as viewed

Reply to an inquiry.

- Agent or agency_owner who received the inquiry can reply
- First reply auto-marks inquiry as responded
- Seeker receives in-platform notification
- Email dispatched to seeker if MAIL_FROM is verified (fail-open)

get.description:
Get replies for an inquiry.

- Seeker who sent the inquiry can read
- Agent/agency_owner who received the inquiry can read
- Admin can read all

schema.title: Response Read Inquiry Replies Api V1 Inquiries  Inquiry Id  Replies  Get

patch.description:
Edit a reply.

- Only the reply author can edit
- Only allowed while viewed_at is NULL (recipient has not seen it yet)
- Updates body and sets edited_at timestamp

get.description:
Count total active inquiries for a property.

- Property owner can view count
- Admins can view any property's count
- Requires active authenticated user

schema.title: Response Count Inquiries For Property Api V1 Inquiries Count  Property Id  Get

get.description:
Count inquiries for a property by status.

- Property owner can view status counts
- Admins can view any property's status counts

schema.title: Response Count Inquiries By Status Api V1 Inquiries Count  Property Id  By Status Get

post.description:
Mark inquiry as responded (deprecated).

This endpoint is deprecated in Phase R. Inquiry status now transitions
to 'responded' automatically when the first reply is posted via
POST /{inquiry_id}/reply/. Use the reply endpoint instead.

- Property owner can mark as responded
- Admins can mark any inquiry as responded

Create a new property ReviewResponse.

- Validates property exists
- Prevents duplicate reviews (one per user per property)
- Rating validation handled by schema

Audit: Tracks reviewer via user_id FK (reviews table has no created_by column)

get.description:
Retrieve all reviews for a specific property.

Public endpoint - returns only non-deleted reviews.
CRUD layer enforces deleted_at IS NULL filtering.

schema.title: Response Read Reviews By Property Api V1 Reviews Property By Property  Property Id  Get

get.description:
Get property ReviewResponse by ID.

Public endpoint - anyone can read reviews.

put.description:
Update a property ReviewResponse.

- Only ReviewResponse author or admin can update
- Rating validation handled by schema

Note: reviews table has no updated_by column per DB schema

delete.description:
Soft delete a property ReviewResponse.

- Only ReviewResponse author or admin can delete
- Sets deleted_at timestamp

Audit: Tracks who deleted via deleted_by (Supabase UUID)

post.description:
Create a new agent ReviewResponse.

- Validates agent exists
- Prevents duplicate reviews (one per user per agent)
- Rating validation handled by schema

Audit: Tracks reviewer via user_id FK (reviews table has no created_by column)

get.description:
Retrieve all reviews for a specific agent.

Public endpoint - returns only non-deleted reviews.

schema.title: Response Read Reviews By Agent Api V1 Reviews Agent By Agent  Agent Id  Get

get.description:
Get agent ReviewResponse by ID.

Public endpoint - anyone can read reviews.

put.description:
Update an agent ReviewResponse.

- Only ReviewResponse author or admin can update

Note: reviews table has no updated_by column per DB schema

delete.description:
Soft delete an agent ReviewResponse.

Audit: Tracks who deleted via deleted_by (Supabase UUID)

post.description:
Create a new agency review.

- Validates agency exists and is approved
- Prevents duplicate reviews (one per user per agency)
- Seeker-only endpoint
- Rating validation handled by schema

get.description:
Retrieve all reviews for a specific agency.

Public endpoint - returns only non-deleted reviews.

schema.title: Response Read Reviews By Agency Api V1 Reviews Agency  Agency Id  Get

get.description:
Retrieve property reviews by the current user.

Returns only non-deleted reviews.

schema.title: Response Read User Property Reviews Api V1 Reviews By User Property  Get

get.description:
Retrieve agent reviews by the current user.

Returns only non-deleted reviews.

schema.title: Response Read User Agent Reviews Api V1 Reviews By User Agent  Get

get.description:
Retrieve agency reviews by the current user.

Returns only non-deleted reviews.

schema.title: Response Read User Agency Reviews Api V1 Reviews By User Agency  Get

own payload makes it obvious in the code and in the API that "editing a
listing" and "making a listing public" are different actions.
